question.text: Under REIT Regulations, what is the maximum value of units to be held by sponsor(s) and sponsor group(s) for compliance with the holding requirements from the 4th year onwards?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under REIT Regulations, the maximum value of units to be held by sponsor(s) and sponsor group(s) for compliance with clauses (ii) to (v) of the holding requirements shall not exceed Rs. 500 crore or such other value as decided by SEBI, based on the latest available NAV of the REIT. **The Trap:** This cap applies only from the 4th year onwards (clauses ii to v), not for the first 3 years when the 15% holding is mandatory without a value cap. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The Rs. 500 crore cap prevents sponsors from being required to hold excessively large positions in very large REITs, while still maintaining meaningful skin in the game.
